attributes.description: Alerts when the request queue depth (`iis.request.queue.count`) exceeds 10 on a pool. Sustained queueing precedes request rejections and timeouts.
attributes.name: [IIS OTel] Request queue depth elevated

investigation_guide.blob:
## IIS request queue depth elevated

### What fired
`iis.request.queue.count > 10` on a pool during the window.

### Why it matters
Queue depth grows when worker threads cannot service requests as fast as they arrive. Sustained queueing causes user-visible latency and, once the configured `requestQueueLimit` is hit, IIS will start rejecting new requests with HTTP 503.

### Triage
1. Identify the affected pool.
2. Correlate with request queue age and rejection counters on the same pool.
3. Inspect CPU and memory of the `w3wp.exe` processes.
4. Look at downstream dependency latency.

### Remediation
- Add worker processes (`maxProcesses`).
- Raise concurrency settings (`maxConcurrentRequestsPerCPU`).
- Fix slow downstreams.

### Tuning
- 10 queue depth threshold; tune to baseline traffic.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-iisreceiver.otel-*
| WHERE iis.request.queue.count IS NOT NULL
| STATS max_queue = MAX(iis.request.queue.count) BY iis.application_pool, host.name
| WHERE max_queue > 10
| SORT max_queue DESC
| LIMIT 20
